trivia_bullets.text: Battle for Zendikar (BFZ, released 2015-10-02) was the first set of the Battle for Zendikar block, depicting the full-scale Eldrazi invasion of Zendikar with the Kor among the main resistance factions. The Kor are a nomadic humanoid race defined by their use of ropes, hooks, and grappling equipment — tools with both practical and sacred-symbolic significance in their culture. [Scryfall API; coolstuffinc.com: "Ecology of the Kor" via WebSearch]
sources: coolstuffinc.com: Ecology of the Kor (WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "The high-pitched whirl of a hook is the only battle cry she needs." [Scryfall] The flavor text directly names the Kor's iconic weapon — the hooked chain — and frames it as sufficient for rallying, which is also the card's mechanical keyword (Rally). The hook-as-sacred-symbol carries specific weight: Kor culture uses a hooked line as a social and religious symbol representing connection between individuals and to Zendikar itself. [Scryfall; coolstuffinc.com: Ecology of the Kor via WebSearch]
sources: coolstuffinc.com: Ecology of the Kor (WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Rally — whenever this creature or another Ally you control enters, creatures you control gain first strike until end of turn — makes Kor Bladewhirl a stackable combat trick in Ally-dense boards. At {1}{W} for a 2/2 it was a limited workhorse in BFZ draft; Pioneer Ally decks have occasionally slotted it for the mass first-strike line. EDHREC rank 13,949. [Scryfall]
Illustrated by Steven Belledin, a prolific MTG artist known for naturalistic portraiture with complex lighting — well-matched to the Kor's textured rope-and-leather aesthetic. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Kor Entanglers
related_cards.note: Sister Kor Ally card in the same set — same race, same Rally keyword, same hook-and-rope visual vocabulary

related_cards.name: Kor Skyfisher
related_cards.note: Classic Kor card from the original Zendikar block; established the hook-and-line visual signature
